document.title: Post-Market Clinical Follow-up Evaluation Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
standards: MDR Annex XIV, Part B – Post-market clinical follow-up
MDCG 2020-7 Post-market clinical follow-up (PMCF) Plan Template - A guide for manufacturers and notified bodies, April 2020
MDCG 2020-8 Post-market clinical follow-up (PMCF) Evaluation Report Template - A guide for manufacturers and notified bodies, April 2020

sourceSummary.oneLine: Rev. 2 PMCF Evaluation Report for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents PMCF activity results for inclusion in the clinical evaluation report and technical documentation under MDR PMCF requirements.
keyPoints: The document states PMCF is a continuous MDR process that updates the clinical evaluation and is addressed in the post-market surveillance plan.
The report covers surveys and screening of scientific literature and other clinical data sources.
No additional impact on clinical safety and performance was identified through PMCF activities.
The device is Class IIb in accordance with rule 10 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745.

concepts.evidence: The Medical Device Regulation (EU) 2017/745 (MDR) considers the post-market clinical follow-up (PMCF) as a continuous process that updates the clinical evaluation

concepts.evidence: The manufacturer shall analyse the findings coming from the activities foreseen in the PMCF plan and document the results in this PMCF evaluation report

evidence.evidenceText: Expected lifetime 5 years

evidence.claim: The PMCF evaluation used surveys and screening scientific literature and other sources of clinical data.
evidence.evidenceText: We conducted PMCF evaluation using the following two methods set in the PMCF Plan, and no additional clinical data was confirmed. C.1: Surveys C.2: Screening scientific literature and other sources of clinical data

evidence.claim: There were no deviations from the PMCF plan.
evidence.evidenceText: The activities were conducted as planned. There were no deviations from the PMCF plan.

evidence.claim: No new critical issues regarding device performance or safety were identified at this PMCF.
evidence.evidenceText: There were no new critical issues regarding the performance or safety of the device at this PMCF.

evidence.claim: Eight healthcare professionals or distributors were surveyed for customer satisfaction.
evidence.evidenceText: A total of 8 healthcare professionals/distributors were surveyed on customer satisfaction

evidence.claim: No customer complaints regarding the product occurred during the survey period.
evidence.evidenceText: There were no customer complaints regarding the product during the survey period.

evidence.claim: The literature search found no additional confirmed literature.
evidence.evidenceText: Literature search : There was no additional confirmed literature.

evidence.claim: Field data searches reported zero FDA recalls, zero Korea MFDS recalls and suspension of sale, and zero UK MHRA alerts and recalls.
evidence.evidenceText: FDA Recall 0; Korea - MFDS Recall and suspension of sale 0; United Kingdom - MHRA Alerts and recalls for drugs and medical devices 0

evidence.claim: The report states no additional impact on clinical safety and performance was identified through PMCF activities.
evidence.evidenceText: No additional impact on clinical safety and performance was identified through PMCF activities.

evidence.claim: The report states no preventive or corrective measures are needed.
evidence.evidenceText: Need for preventive and/or corrective measures None

evidence.claim: The first PMCF evaluation report date is December 31, 2024 and the planned second report date is December 2025.
evidence.evidenceText: Reported date of the first report: December 31, 2024 Planned date of the second report: December 2025 Timeline: Once a year

reviewFlags.reason: The document references collected data counts using placeholders such as 'X related Survey' and 'X related Literature', so those counts should not be treated as extracted facts.
Authority/jurisdiction is based on MDR references in the document; no notified body, certificate number, or CE certificate status is stated.
Section numbering has an internal inconsistency: CXDI-31 of Canon is labeled as section 5.1 after section 5.2.
